sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Now the two L-shapes are touching! The black L-shape and the dark gray L-shape are now adjacent. The black L-shape is on the left, and the dark gray is on the right, and they're touching at the boundary between them. Let me check the current boundaries to see what happened.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
after_seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in after_seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] in [5, 6, 12]:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! Now I can see:
- id=5 (black L-shape): boundary=[[15, 24], [15, 32], [23, 32], [23, 30], [18, 30], [17, 29], [17, 24]]
  - The black L-shape moved right again - from col 21-29 to col 24-32. It's now touching the sky blue divider (col 30-32).
- id=6 (dark gray L-shape): boundary=[[15, 33], [15, 38], [17, 38], [17, 33]]
  The dark gray L-shape shrank from 45 pixels to 18 pixels. It's now just a 2x5 rectangle! The vertical part of the L-shape disappeared!
- id=12: color=S, pixels=117, boundary=[[24, 30], [24, 32], [62, 32], [62, 30]]
  This is the sky blue divider below the L-shapes.
So when the black L-shape moved right and touched the dark gray L-shape, the dark gray L-shape got "absorbed" or "merged" - it shrank to just the overlapping portion.
The goal seems to be to be to move the black L-shape to match the dark gray L-shape. Let me try RIGHT again to see if the black L-shape to match the dark gray L-shape.
Let me try RIGHT again to see if the black L-shape can continue moving.
